model: Analysis based on mNAV time-series data

analysis.current_status: As of 2026-04-02, Strategy's mNAV stands at 0.89x, representing a 11.3% discount to its Bitcoin holdings. This means the market values the company at $45.3B while its 762,099 BTC are worth $51.1B.
analysis.trend_analysis: Over the past 30 trading days, mNAV has declined 13.8% (from 1.03x to 0.89x), while BTC price dropped 7.8% (from $72,720 to $67,020). The mNAV compression outpaced BTC's decline, suggesting increasing market skepticism about the leveraged Bitcoin treasury model.
analysis.historical_context: The mNAV peaked at 2.25x on 2025-05-02 during a period of strong BTC momentum and investor enthusiasm for the DAT strategy. It has since compressed by 61%, crossing below the critical 1.0x threshold on approximately late 2025. The lowest recorded mNAV was 0.84x on 2026-02-05.
analysis.investor_implications: An mNAV below 1.0x indicates the market values MSTR's equity below its Bitcoin holdings, which theoretically makes the accretive dilution flywheel unsustainable — new share issuances at a discount to NAV dilute existing shareholders' BTC-per-share. However, this discount also means investors can gain Bitcoin exposure at a discount through MSTR stock compared to buying BTC directly. The current 11.3% discount may attract value-oriented investors who believe mNAV will revert above 1.0x.
analysis.correlation_insight: mNAV exhibits strong pro-cyclical behavior with Bitcoin. During the observed period, mNAV amplified BTC movements — rising faster than BTC in bull phases (MSTR beta ~1.3-1.4x) and compressing more sharply in downturns. The 30-day data shows mNAV fell 13.8% while BTC fell only 7.8%, confirming this leveraged exposure characteristic.
